_comment: Layer 2 retrieval base. Chunked from Phase 1 Artifact 2 (BI Brief) per A6 §A.1.2. Loaded into memory at server start; matched against extracted entities + numbers per A6 §A.2 triggers. Step 5 ships a curated subset (most-likely-to-be-mentioned content); Step 9 polish can expand if dry-run shows retrieval gaps.

chunks.source_section: A2 §7 — Sales Practice / The stalled-deal pool
chunks.summary: $20–30M+ of Hakai BC pipeline stalled 8–16 months at the 40% Qualified stage. Named deals include Calgary Stampede Big Four ($888K, 14mo), Precon Manufacturing ($826K, 16mo), DIRTT ($635K, 14mo), Garibaldi Glass ($2M, 10mo), Allen Town ($1M, 8mo), Westfine Meats ($1.2M, 5mo), Bonnybrook ($505K), City of Airdrie Twin Arenas ($650K, 14mo). Pre-acquisition Hakai had no documented qualification discipline.

chunks.source_section: A2 §5 + §7 — HubSpot portal state, May 8 audit findings
chunks.summary: May 8 HubSpot audit: 8,842 open deals reported; 7,285 attributed to Stacy Haakonson by Dec 2024 migration default; only 1,367 (15.5%) owned by an active sales team member. 41,938 total contacts. 0 contacts ever enrolled in a HubSpot sequence. 25–30 Salesforce-era properties to archive. 94% of open deals untouched 30 days. Forecast overstatement $5–12M/qtr. Pipelines: Utility-Scale (50% at Qualified vs. 3–8% actual), Hakai BC ID 667712064 (60% at Qualified vs. 30–35% actual; stage ID 979641939 holds $20–30M+ stalled), Commercial Rooftop (incomplete stage architecture). No Site Assessed stage. February 19, 2025 mass-stage-update broke time-in-stage calculations.

Greg Sauer owns 301 open deals personally. 90-day activity: 192 calls, 3,084 emails, 7 tasks (440:1 email-to-task ratio). 112 of his deals (37%) untouched in 30 days. May 11 reorg sheet frustrations tab explicitly names: 'consulting siloed w/ Greg.' Greg's own quote May 8: 'We're paying for something we're not using.' The C&I sales bench (Bryce, Rob MacInnis, Robert Silver, Matt Simard) has no system-level visibility into Greg's deal load.

Stage probabilities are residential-era and structurally overstate pipeline by $5–12M per quarter. Worked example: $30M+ at Hakai BC 60% = $18M weighted today, but calibrated to actual 30–35% conversion weights to $9–10.5M ($7–9M overstatement on Hakai alone). Utility-Scale 50% configured vs. actual 3–8% RFP win rate (25–35 pts overstated). David Vonesch runs a manual quarterly forecasting cycle, polling each rep individually because he can't trust the HubSpot number. 30-Day Action #5: recalibrate stage probabilities against actual win rates by June 5.

96.3% of 18,853 companies in the database have no industry tag — segment-level win rate, deal velocity, and pipeline coverage cannot be reported today. SkyFire's own $12.15M 2026 C&I target is split by geography (YYC $9.86M / YEG $1.24M / YLW $1.04M), not by segment. Only 4 of 15+ named segments have GA4 audiences (Residential 17,546 / Commercial 12,950 / Agriculture 93 / First Nations 5). No audience configured for REIT, cold storage, mining, cannabis, data centres, military, IPP, aerospace, wilderness lodging, multi-family builders, or general/electrical contractors. 30-Day Action #3 builds company_segment field + 18-option picklist + top-500 backfill by May 28.

chunks.source_section: A2 §6 — Stingray paid social + attribution loop
chunks.summary: GA4 Jan 1 – May 16, 2026: Paid Other channel (overwhelmingly paid_social_stingray) — 6,467 sessions, 7 key events, 0.11% conversion rate. Same window Organic Search: 8,187 sessions, 1,048 key events, 12.80% rate. The Qualified leads and Converted leads GA4 metrics read 0 across every week from 0000 through 0019 of 2026 YTD. C&I Marketing Strategy doc references Stingray Connect as the digital ad partner with weekly tweaks and ongoing A/B testing — assumes Stingray is delivering against KPI; the data says otherwise. Stingray monthly spend not visible in project files; typical paid-social retainer plus ad-spend ~CAD $5–15K/month.

Hakai Energy Solutions acquired January 1, 2026. 15-year-old company based on Vancouver Island. Pre-acquisition CEO Jason Jackson now EVP Strategy & Business Development at SkyFire. Headcount post-acquisition ~200 (up from ~97 pre-acquisition). Hakai BC pipeline (HubSpot ID 667712064) holds 3,641 deals, largest single deal NRStor Natuashish at $40M. Hakai C&I integration: 2 on C&I side (Rob MacInnis provisioned May 4 with sales-pro seat + Project Director title; Jason Jackson), 3–4 on residential side (Alexandra Poepl, Piers Bonifant, Chris Den Tandt provisioned May 7). Hakai's BC capability is hybrid AC-coupled, off-grid and microgrid systems for remote communities; Calvert Island Ecological Observatory was the reference project from 2010 onward. Curtis Buxton creating Hakai BC team in HubSpot by May 14.

SkyFire Energy Inc. 25-year-old solar EPC and O&M firm. HQ 4038A 7 Street SE, Calgary, AB T2G 2Y8. First install (Alberta's third grid-tied) by David Kelly in 2001 under Sedmek Inc. Formally incorporated as SkyFire Energy in 2008 by David Kelly, Tim Schulhauser, David Vonesch. Offices: Calgary HQ, Edmonton, Regina, West Kelowna. Post-Hakai (Jan 1 2026): Vancouver Island (Comox/Cumberland), Vancouver coastal, Victoria. Employee-owned via ESOP since 2018. B Corp recertification 103.7 vs. median 50.9. Other certifications: COR, Climate Smart (since 2019), Amicus Solar Cooperative (first non-US member 2016), Tesla Powerwall Certified Installer, Leadership Accord on Gender Diversity. CEO David Vonesch on CanREA board. 250+ MWp PV installed; 8,500+ grid-connected systems; 2+ GWp serviced under O&M (230+ MWp under full-scope O&M contract). 8 provinces/territories. 20+ Canadian utility partners.

Named competitor set from SkyFire's own C&I Marketing Strategy doc: Alberta — Kuby, Rocky Mountain Solar, Altapro. BC — Inferno, Altapro, IPS. Utility-scale — Borea, GP Joule, PCL. Broader Western Canada (per master dossier): Polaron Solartech (Toronto, residential-heavy, 12,500+ installs), Kuby Renewable Energy (Edmonton, 47–55 employees, closest AB C&I rival), EVOLVsolar (Calgary/Edmonton), Great Canadian Solar (Edmonton), Solvest Inc. (Whitehorse, 65–72 employees, northern/remote/mining specialty).

chunks.source_section: A2 §10 — What's already been decided / 30-day plan
chunks.summary: Five 30-Day Plan actions approved at the May 8 sync: (1) Reassign Stacy's 7,285 deals — Curtis Buxton — May 21. (2) Sweep 8,000 overdue close dates — Greg + rep delegation — May 28. (3) Build company_segment field + 18-option picklist + top-500 backfill — Joey (build) + Curtis (deploy) + Greg/segment directors (validate) — May 28. (4) Move Robert Silver to All C&I Sales team; create Hakai BC team — Curtis — May 14. (5) Recalibrate stage probabilities against actual win rates — Joey (analysis) + Curtis (configure) — June 5. Implicit sixth: Joey produces property-cleanup how-to guide; Curtis + James McPhail execute in-house. SOW: Option B, $9,900/month × 4 months = $39,600, May 1 – August 31. June 9–10 Bragg Creek C&I Sales Retreat confirmed.
